methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Radian Forge is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jeff Yeager that has raised $3M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable, though the broader robotics and manufacturing sector contains 11 identifiable peers.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using total capital raised of $3M as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $8M. This method captures the standard valuation expansion expected as the company advances from seed funding. Peer-median analysis is not applicable; while 11 same-sector peers exist, none meet the narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables. Constructing a meaningful median from the broader sector would introduce material bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6M to $9M, with a median point of $8M. This range reflects a 15% discount and 15% premium around the stage-progression midpoint, accounting for execution risk and the absence of narrowly comparable financials. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer benchmarking data at seed stage within the robotics sector and the reliance on a single methodology. Further due diligence on product-market fit and runway metrics would refine this estimate.
